sections.heading: Surveillance
sections.content: Surveillance for cutaneous lymphomas involves clinical follow-up, imaging, laboratory monitoring, and supportive care. Clinical follow-up schedules are not standardized but are tailored to disease subtype and stage. For indolent PC-BCL, regular skin exams and palpation of lymph nodes are sufficient, with imaging only if clinically indicated. For MF/SS, follow-up includes complete skin examination, palpation of peripheral lymph nodes, and assessment of organomegaly at each visit. Laboratory monitoring includes complete blood count (CBC) with differential, comprehensive metabolic panel, and lactate dehydrogenase (LDH). For patients with blood involvement (B1 or B2), flow cytometry to quantitate abnormal T-cells is recommended, along with clonal T-cell receptor (TCR) gene rearrangement studies if blood involvement is suspected [22]. Imaging strategy: integrated whole-body FDG-PET/CT (preferred) or C/A/P CT with contrast is recommended for staging in MF/SS for patients with T3/T4 disease, LCT, folliculotropic MF, palpable adenopathy, or abnormal laboratory studies. For PC-ALCL, C/A/P CT or FDG-PET/CT is recommended at diagnosis. In LyP, imaging is only done if there is suspicion of systemic involvement by an associated lymphoma [21]. Supportive follow-up includes assessment of pruritus, infection risk, and quality of life. Patients on phototherapy should be monitored for actinic keratoses, non-melanoma skin cancers, and melanoma. For patients on alemtuzumab, CMV PCR monitoring every 2–3 weeks is essential [17].

timeline.title: At diagnosis
timeline.body: For PC-ALCL: C/A/P CT or FDG-PET/CT. For MF/SS with T3/T4 disease, LCT, folliculotropic MF, palpable adenopathy, or abnormal laboratory studies: integrated whole-body FDG-PET/CT (preferred) or C/A/P CT with contrast.

timeline.title: Each visit (regular follow-up)
timeline.body: Complete skin examination, palpation of peripheral lymph nodes, assessment of organomegaly. Laboratory monitoring: CBC with differential, comprehensive metabolic panel, LDH. For patients with blood involvement (B1 or B2): flow cytometry to quantitate abnormal T-cells, and clonal TCR gene rearrangement studies if blood involvement is suspected. Supportive follow-up: assessment of pruritus, infection risk, and quality of life.

timeline.title: For indolent PC-BCL
timeline.body: Regular skin exams and palpation of lymph nodes. Imaging only if clinically indicated.

timeline.title: For LyP
timeline.body: Imaging only if there is suspicion of systemic involvement by an associated lymphoma.

timeline.title: During phototherapy
timeline.body: Monitor for actinic keratoses, non-melanoma skin cancers, and melanoma.
